detail.summary: A skill-by-skill structure showing what each skill does, when it is invoked, and what outputs it leaves.
detail.skillsDescription: Definition and execution points by skill

cardItems.title: orchestrator-designer
cardItems.description: A skill for designing multi-agent collaboration flows, including decomposition, role assignment, routing, loop control, and merge rules.

sections.title: What the Skill Does
sections.description: Tasks this skill directly designs and decides.

items.title: Task Decomposition and Role Assignment
items.description: Breaks a user request into work units and assigns the agent role needed for each stage.

items.title: Parallel Result Merge
items.description: Compares or merges parallel agent outputs and hands them to the next stage.

items.title: Loop and Termination Rules
items.description: Defines where to loop back when output is insufficient and when to terminate the flow.

sections.title: Skill Patterns
sections.description: Recurring structural patterns used when applying this skill.

items.title: Fan-out then Converge
items.description: Multiple agents process in parallel, then results are gathered back into a single output.

items.title: Conditional Routing
items.description: The next agent depends on the intermediate result.

items.title: Feedback Loop
items.description: Returns to a previous stage and re-runs when the result falls short.

sections.title: Skill Principles
sections.description: Guidelines for using this skill consistently and safely.

items.title: Avoid Deep Nesting
items.description: The main orchestrator owns the full flow instead of stacking deep nested layers.

items.title: Define Merge Criteria First
items.description: Decides what counts as a good result before comparing or merging multiple outputs.

items.title: Cap Loop Length
items.description: Pre-sets retry counts and termination conditions to prevent infinite loops.

sections.title: Skill Outputs
sections.description: Core elements left in the final output after this skill runs.

items.title: Flow Overview
items.description: Defines the orchestrator overview and stage composition first.

items.title: Branch, Loop, and Merge Diagram
items.description: Visualizes agent relationships, branching, loops, and merges with diagrams.

items.title: Operating Rules
items.description: Specifies when to hand off, when to retry, how to merge, and when to terminate.

cardItems.title: blog-post-mogiyoon
cardItems.description: Turns rough drafts, scattered notes, or topic-only inputs into Velog (velog.io/@mogiyoon) posts. Matches the user's plain-form Korean voice to a chosen category tone (gansal, gochal, debugging, makeismylife) and saves the result as a Markdown file.

sections.title: What the Skill Does
sections.description: Tasks this skill directly designs and decides.

items.title: Genre Confirmation
items.description: Confirms one of four categories (gansal, gochal, debugging, makeismylife) by user statement, content inference, or explicit agreement.

items.title: Topic Extraction
items.description: Splits broad input into topic candidates and narrows to a single message per post.

items.title: Input Shape Detection
items.description: Chooses the writing mode based on whether the input is a rough draft, a finished-post rewrite, or a topic-only request.

items.title: Tone and Style Application
items.description: Reads common-style and the selected category guide, then rewrites the draft in plain-form Korean.

sections.title: Skill Patterns
sections.description: Recurring structural patterns used when applying this skill.

items.title: Genre → Topic → Input Shape
items.description: Confirms category, topic, and input shape with the user before writing.

items.title: Primary + Secondary Category
items.description: When mixing tones, picks one main category and borrows phrasing from the secondary.

items.title: Topic Candidates → Single Pick
items.description: When multiple topics surface, lists candidates and lets the user choose one.

sections.title: Skill Principles
sections.description: Guidelines for using this skill consistently and safely.

items.title: Plain-Form Unification
items.description: Uses ~다 / ~한다 endings and never the formal honorific ~습니다.

items.title: Preserve User Voice
items.description: Keeps the user's self-deprecating notes and the mix of short and long paragraphs.

items.title: Self-Check Before Handoff
items.description: Reviews tone match, paragraph rhythm, and intro/outro naturalness before delivering.

items.title: Fact-Checking First
items.description: Never invents the user's experiences and asks for missing information instead.

sections.title: Skill Outputs
sections.description: Core elements left in the final output after this skill runs.

items.title: Markdown File (.md)
items.description: Saves the result file to a user-specified path or /tmp/.

items.title: Filename Convention
items.description: Saves as {primary_category}-{english-kebab-topic}.md.

items.title: One-Line Summary
items.description: Hands off the core message in one line plus the file path link.

cardItems.title: register-portfolio-project
cardItems.description: Registers a completed external project into the mogiyoon portfolio site. Extracts metadata, gets user confirmation, generates ko/en i18n, auto-detects claudeInfo, copies images, edits the related portfolio files, then forks a feature branch from develop and commits.

sections.title: What the Skill Does
sections.description: Tasks this skill directly designs and decides.

items.title: Auto-Extract Project Info
items.description: Reads package.json, README, git log, and image candidates in parallel to collect metadata.

items.title: claudeInfo Auto-Detection
items.description: Inspects .claude/agents/, orchestrator traces, and Co-Authored-By patterns to determine method (direct/harness/orchestrator).

items.title: Korean Tone Normalization
items.description: Unifies endings to ~함 / ~됨 / ~임 and substitutes banned expressions.

items.title: ko → en Auto Translation
items.description: Generates the English i18n once Korean is confirmed, mirroring the same structure.

items.title: Portfolio Repo File Generation
items.description: Writes data, locales, list, card translations, and image assets across six files.

sections.title: Skill Patterns
sections.description: Recurring structural patterns used when applying this skill.

items.title: Extract → Review → Write → Branch
items.description: Fixes the order of auto-extract, batch user review, file generation, and branch creation.

items.title: ko First, en Auto-Translated
items.description: Confirms Korean i18n first, then auto-generates the English version.

items.title: Batch User Review
items.description: A single AskUserQuestion call confirms id, subtitle, projectType, techStack, and features at once.

sections.title: Skill Principles
sections.description: Guidelines for using this skill consistently and safely.

items.title: Fork From develop
items.description: Creates feat/add-{id}, never works on main directly.

items.title: Safe Automation Boundary
items.description: pull, push, and PR creation only proceed after explicit user approval.

items.title: Source Project Is Read-Only
items.description: Images are copied, never modified at the source project.

items.title: Strict Tone Guide
items.description: Applies CLAUDE.md's ending and banned-expression rules to all Korean text.

sections.title: Skill Outputs
sections.description: Core elements left in the final output after this skill runs.

items.title: New Data File
items.description: Creates public/data/projects/{id}.json.

items.title: ko / en i18n
items.description: Creates public/locales/ko·en/projects/project-{id}.json.

items.title: List and Card Entries
items.description: Appends entries to projects-list.json and ko/en projects.json.

items.title: Image Assets
items.description: Copies public/images/{camelCaseProjectName}/Icon, appGif, and overview/* files.

items.title: Feature Branch + Commit
items.description: Forks feat/add-{id} from develop and creates the commit.

detail.summary: An MCP index connecting Unity and Blender work contexts.

harness.title: Harness
harness.description: A set of repeatable AI implementation and evaluation harnesses

detail.summary: Harness structures with role separation and test, evaluation, and review loops
detail.patternsDescription: A harness index grouped by implementation, validation, regression, and documentation sync purposes.

patternsItems.title: TheHabit Implementation Harness
patternsItems.description: Architect plans, Coder implements, failed tests trigger a Debugger + Coder fix loop, and Reviewer closes the flow.

stepDetails.action: Turns the issue into a concrete implementation plan.
Implements the planned change in code.
Runs the go/no-go quality gate.
Explains failures and routes fixes back.
Checks the final diff and closes the run.

loops.label: When tests fail, the flow returns from debug to implementation.

groups.title: Runs
points: Feature implementation tasks from a concrete plan or issue.
Architect, Coder, Debugger, and Reviewer roles in order.

groups.title: Controls
points: Role handoff order and retry path after failed tests.
Debug-to-implementation loop boundaries.

groups.title: Checks
points: Automated test result before review.
Reviewer pass before the run is considered complete.

groups.title: Artifacts
points: Changed files, test logs, review notes, and final implementation summary.

patternsItems.title: Dev Harness
patternsItems.description: A spec-driven implementation harness that loads a JSON spec, prepares a branch, runs Codex, tests the result, and can finish with commit and PR creation.

stepDetails.action: Reads the run contract from spec JSON.
Prepares the target branch and working tree.
Implements the requested change from the spec.
Runs the checks required by the spec.
Packages commit and PR context.

samples.title: Spec JSON shape
samples.description: The harness reads this contract before it prepares a branch or starts Codex.
samples.code:
{
  "id": "habit-reminder-flow",
  "branch": "feat/habit-reminder-flow",
  "goal": "Add reminder copy and validation to the habit creation flow",
  "entry": "src/features/habits",
  "checks": ["npm run lint", "npm run build"],
  "doneWhen": ["copy is localized", "validation errors are tested", "PR summary lists changed files"]
}

groups.title: Runs
points: JSON spec that describes the task, branch, and completion criteria.
Codex implementation session against the prepared branch.

groups.title: Controls
points: Branch setup, working tree state, command policy, and commit / PR sequence.
Stop points for test failures or dirty-state checks.

groups.title: Checks
points: Build, lint, or test commands listed by the spec.
Git diff review before commit or PR.

groups.title: Artifacts
points: Commit, PR body, command output, and implementation notes.

patternsItems.title: Eval Harness
patternsItems.description: A validation harness that runs 50+ scenarios through the live pipeline to verify routing, tool usage, and result status.

stepDetails.action: Runs saved scenarios through the pipeline.
Captures route, tool, status, and output data.
Checks route and status expectations.

groups.title: Runs
points: Scenario set with 50+ requests through the live pipeline.
Routing, tool, and status checks for each scenario.

groups.title: Controls
points: Scenario order, expected status schema, and result collection format.
Repeatable run record for comparison across changes.

groups.title: Checks
points: Tool selection, route correctness, output status, and gap detection.
Pass / fail aggregation across scenarios.

groups.title: Artifacts
points: Harness results table, failure list, logs, and follow-up issues.

patternsItems.title: Improve Harness
patternsItems.description: A self-improvement loop where a User agent generates requests, an Evaluator grades outcomes, and a Developer fixes failed items before regression checks.

stepDetails.action: Generates weak-spot requests.
Runs the current system.
Scores results against the rubric.
Fixes failures and records changes.
Checks older scenarios still pass.

loops.label: If regression checks still fail, the flow goes back into execution and evaluation.

groups.title: Runs
points: Generated user requests and previous failure cases.
Developer fixes followed by regression execution.

groups.title: Controls
points: Evaluator scoring rubric, retry limits, and fix-loop entry rules.
Regression gate before accepting improvements.

groups.title: Checks
points: Evaluator grade, fixed-item verification, and regression pass.
No new failures in previously passing cases.

groups.title: Artifacts
points: Improvement report, failing prompts, fix notes, and regression results.

patternsItems.title: Doc Harness
patternsItems.description: A documentation sync harness that analyzes code-doc drift, updates docs, then verifies and re-verifies the result.

stepDetails.action: Finds where code behavior and docs drifted apart.
Updates docs to match the current code path.
Checks the docs against code and examples.
Runs a stricter second pass before completion.

loops.label: If mismatches remain after verification, the flow returns to updating docs.

groups.title: Runs
points: Code-document drift analysis against changed files.
Documentation update and verification pass.

groups.title: Controls
points: Analyze-update-verify order and re-check trigger.
Scope boundaries for files that docs should describe.

groups.title: Checks
points: Doc accuracy against code, missing behavior, and stale examples.
Higher-quality re-check before completion.

groups.title: Artifacts
points: Updated docs, verification notes, mismatch list, and re-check summary.

detail.abstractDescription: Architecture principles shared across the harness system.

abstractItems.title: Role separation
abstractItems.description: Planning, implementation, evaluation, and review are split instead of being overloaded onto one agent.

abstractItems.title: Built-in quality gates
abstractItems.description: Testing, review, or scenario evaluation always follows execution as an explicit gate.

abstractItems.title: Feedback loops
abstractItems.description: Failure does not terminate the flow immediately; feedback is routed back into the implementation stage.

abstractItems.title: Repeatability
abstractItems.description: Artifacts such as tasks.yaml, spec JSON, logs, and harness-results make the loops reusable across runs.
